title: Morning Sickness and Herbal Tea: 10 Remedies to Try
intro_desc: Herbal teas for morning sickness are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate the symptoms of nausea and vomiting experienced by pregnant women during the first trimester. These teas work by soothing the stomach, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal teas for morning sickness include Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Foeniculum vulgare, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Mentha x piperita, Angelica archangelica, Matricaria chamomilla, Piper nigrum, Rosmarinus officinalis, and Cinnamomum verum. If left untreated, morning sickness can lead to dehydration, weight loss, and fatigue, negatively impacting daily life, work, and relationships.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, has been used for centuries to help with morning sickness during pregnancy. When you drink ginger tea, the active ingredients in the ginger, called gingerols and shogaols, are released into your body. These ingredients can help to calm your stomach and reduce the nausea and vomiting that often come with morning sickness. They do this by blocking the action of a chemical in the brain that causes nausea and vomiting, called serotonin. By blocking serotonin, ginger tea can help to reduce the symptoms of morning sickness and make it easier for you to feel better and get on with your day. Ginger tea is also easy to make and can be sipped throughout the day to provide relief from morning sickness. Many pregnant women have tried ginger tea and reported a reduction in their symptoms, making it a popular choice for those who are struggling with morning sickness.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol, also known as chamomile, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease morning sickness by reducing nausea and stomach discomfort.

Shogaol has been shown to be effective in alleviating morning sickness due to its ability to inhibit the action of a chemical called dopamine, which is thought to play a role in triggering nausea and vomiting during pregnancy.

Zingerone helps alleviate morning sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the stomach and reduce nausea, making it a promising natural remedy for pregnant women suffering from morning sickness.

Volatile oils, also known as German chamomile, are good for morning sickness because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea and vomiting by calming the digestive system and promoting relaxation.
Zingiberene is good for morning sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting by soothing the stomach and digestive system.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for morning sickness
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea, which can help with morning sickness, start by gathering dried chamomile flowers. You can buy these at a health food store or online. Next, place one teaspoon of the dried flowers in a tea infuser or a small piece of cheesecloth, and tie it with a string to keep it from falling out. Then, boil some water in a kettle and pour it over the flowers in a cup. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flowers to release their medicinal properties into the water. After the steeping time, carefully remove the flowers from the water and discard them. Strain the tea into another cup if needed, and your chamomile tea is ready to drink. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups of this tea per day to help alleviate morning sickness symptoms. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to drink it warm or at room temperature for maximum effectiveness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is a natural remedy for morning sickness during pregnancy. This tea has been used for centuries to help alleviate nausea and vomiting. The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain special compounds that help calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach. When consumed as a tea, these compounds can ease morning sickness symptoms, allowing pregnant women to feel more comfortable and at ease. The anti-inflammatory properties in Ginkgo biloba tea can also help relax the stomach muscles, reducing the likelihood of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, this tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, further supporting overall health during pregnancy. Drinking Ginkgo biloba tea in moderation may help pregnant women manage their morning sickness symptoms, but it's essential to consult a healthcare provider before using any new supplements or herbal remedies.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for morning sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting by calming the stomach and relaxing the digestive system.
Bilobalide is good for morning sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea and vomiting by soothing the stomach and promoting relaxation.

Kaempferol is good for morning sickness because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce nausea and vomiting in pregnant women by soothing the stomach and reducing stress.
Isorhapontigenin is good for morning sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea and vomiting by soothing the stomach and relaxing the digestive system.
Ginkgolide B is good for morning sickness because it has been shown to help reduce nausea and vomiting by inhibiting the action of serotonin, a chemical that can trigger these symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for morning sickness
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea that can help with morning sickness, you will need dried chamomile flowers, a pot, a tea infuser, and a cup. First, boil some water in the pot and then place the dried chamomile flowers in the tea infuser. Once the water is boiling, carefully pour it over the chamomile flowers in the infuser. Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes, which will help release the active ingredients from the flowers. After the steeping time, lift the infuser out of the pot and pour the tea into a cup. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's best to drink the tea without any additives to get the full benefits of the chamomile. Once you've made the tea, you can drink it as needed to help alleviate morning sickness symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a natural remedy that may help with morning sickness. The tea made from its seeds has been used for centuries to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. Fennel seeds contain compounds that ease digestion and reduce inflammation in the stomach. When consumed as a tea, these compounds are absorbed quickly into the body, helping to calm the stomach and reduce the symptoms of morning sickness. Drinking fennel tea in the morning may also help regulate bowel movements and reduce bloating. Additionally, fennel has a mild anise flavor, which may be soothing for some women experiencing morning sickness. As a natural remedy, fennel tea may be a good alternative to other morning sickness treatments that contain harsh chemicals.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anethole is good for morning sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting by calming the stomach and promoting a sense of relaxation.
Foeniculin is good for morning sickness because it helps to reduce nausea and vomiting by calming the stomach and nerves, which can help pregnant women to feel better.
Polyphenols are good for morning sickness because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting by soothing the digestive system and calming the mind.

remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal teas for morning sickness
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal teas that help with morning sickness, start by gathering dried chamomile flowers, either by purchasing them at a health food store or by drying fresh flowers at home. Begin by boiling water in a teapot and then adding one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers for every eight ounces of water. Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes, then strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth. Discard the solids and add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. Once the tea has cooled slightly, drink it slowly to help alleviate morning sickness symptoms. It is recommended to drink this tea in moderation, about one to two cups per day, as directed by a healthcare professional. Drinking this tea before breakfast may help ease morning sickness, and it can also be consumed during the day to provide additional relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is a natural remedy that may help alleviate morning sickness. This is because the tea contains compounds called flavonoids and glycyrrhizin, which have anti-inflammatory properties. Morning sickness, often caused by hormonal changes during pregnancy, can lead to nausea and vomiting. The anti-inflammatory properties in licorice root tea may help reduce inflammation in the stomach, which can ease these symptoms. Additionally, the tea has been shown to help regulate digestive enzymes, which can also contribute to morning sickness. By reducing inflammation and regulating digestive enzymes, Glycyrrhiza glabra tea may provide relief from morning sickness and help pregnant women feel more comfortable during the early stages of pregnancy.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for morning sickness because it helps to reduce nausea and vomiting by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory that soothes the stomach and alleviates digestive discomfort.
Flavonoids, also known as chamomile, are good for morning sickness because they have a calming effect on the stomach and can help to reduce nausea and vomiting.
Saponins are good for morning sickness because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea and vomiting by calming the stomach and promoting digestion.

Polyphenols are good for morning sickness because they help to reduce nausea and vomiting by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, thereby alleviating the symptoms of pregnancy-related morning sickness.
Triterpenoids are good for morning sickness because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help calm the stomach and reduce nausea.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for morning sickness
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea for morning sickness, start by gathering dried chamomile flowers or buying them from a health food store. Begin by boiling one cup of water in a teapot, then add one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers to the boiling water. Allow the flowers to steep for five to seven minutes to release their medicinal properties. You can also add a little honey to the tea if you prefer a sweeter taste. Once the tea has steeped, strain it into a cup and discard the flowers. Drink the tea slowly and enjoy its calming effects. It is recommended to drink two to three cups of chamomile tea per day to help alleviate morning sickness symptoms. Note that pregnant women should consult their doctor before consuming any herbal remedy, including chamomile tea, as a precautionary measure.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica tea has been used for centuries to help with morning sickness. The active ingredients in this herbal tea, called angelica, have a calming effect on the stomach and can help reduce nausea and vomiting. Drinking a warm cup of angelica tea in the morning may also help ease digestive issues and regulate bowel movements, making it easier to stomach food. This is because angelica has natural properties that slow down digestion, giving the body time to process food properly. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce the swelling in the stomach, which can contribute to morning sickness. Some people also find that the sweet, earthy flavor of angelica tea is soothing and can help calm the mind and body, further reducing morning sickness symptoms.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Coumarins are good for morning sickness because they help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea by slowing down the movement of food through the digestive system.

Phenolic acids are good for morning sickness because they help reduce nausea and vomiting by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, calming the stomach and alleviating symptoms of pregnancy-related discomfort.

Volatile oils are good for morning sickness because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the stomach and reduce nausea, making them a natural remedy for easing symptoms of morning sickness.
Ligustilide is good for morning sickness because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nausea and vomiting by calming the digestive system and promoting relaxation.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea to help with morning sickness, start by gathering dried chamomile flowers. You can buy these at a health food store or online. Next, measure out one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers for every eight ounces of water you plan to use. Heat the water in a kettle or pot, then pour it over the chamomile flowers in a cup or teapot. Let the mixture steep for five to seven minutes, allowing the flowers to release their healing properties into the water. After the steeping time is up, strain the tea into another cup to remove the chamomile flowers. Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink the tea whenever morning sickness is a problem. It's recommended to drink the tea in small amounts, about 1/2 cup, two to three times a day, to help alleviate symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum tea, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, has been used for centuries to help alleviate morning sickness. One reason for this is its ability to stabilize blood sugar levels. When blood sugar drops, women often experience nausea and dizziness, common symptoms of morning sickness. Drinking Cinnamomum verum tea can help regulate blood sugar, reducing these symptoms. Another reason is its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the stomach and reduce discomfort. Cinnamomum verum tea also contains compounds that have a calming effect on the body, which can help reduce stress and anxiety often associated with morning sickness. Additionally, its warm and comforting nature can help ease nausea and promote a sense of well-being.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cinnamaldehyde is good for morning sickness because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nausea and vomiting by soothing the stomach and calming the nervous system.
Tannins are good for morning sickness because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the stomach and reduce nausea.
Polyphenols are good for morning sickness because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea and vomiting by soothing the stomach and promoting relaxation.
Eugenol is good for morning sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties and can help relax the stomach muscles, reducing nausea and vomiting.

remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal teas for morning sickness
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea to help with morning sickness, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried chamomile flowers and a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Begin by boiling fresh water in a kettle or on the stovetop, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Next, add one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers to the tea infuser or directly to the cup. Pour the simmering water over the chamomile flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and medicinal properties to infuse into the water. Once the tea has steeped, carefully strain the liquid into a separate cup using the tea infuser or a spoon, and discard the chamomile flowers. Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea is now ready to drink; it's recommended to consume it in moderation, about 1-2 cups per day, to help alleviate morning sickness symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tea, also known as peppermint tea, is often recommended for morning sickness. This is because peppermint has a calming effect on the stomach, which can help to ease nausea and vomiting. When you drink peppermint tea, the oils in the peppermint can help to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation, making it easier for the body to digest food. Peppermint also contains a compound called menthol, which can help to clear the sinuses and ease congestion, which can be a common symptom of morning sickness. Additionally, peppermint has natural antispasmodic properties, which can help to reduce the frequency and severity of stomach cramps and contractions, making it easier to manage morning sickness. Drinking peppermint tea can be a comforting and soothing way to manage symptoms and get through the day.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for morning sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea and vomiting.
Menthol is good for morning sickness because it helps to reduce nausea and vomiting by relaxing the stomach muscles and improving digestion.
Polyphenols are good for morning sickness because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea and vomiting.
Menthone is good for morning sickness because it helps to calm the stomach and reduce nausea by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic agent.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea that helps with morning sickness, start by gathering dried chamomile flowers or purchasing them from a health food store. If using fresh flowers, dry them by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once you have dried chamomile, measure out one teaspoon of flowers for every cup of boiling water you plan to use. Next, boil water in a kettle or on the stovetop and then pour it over the chamomile flowers in a cup or teapot. Allow the flowers to steep for 5-7 minutes, which will help release their medicinal properties. After the steeping time is up, strain the tea into another cup using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the flowers. Your Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea is now ready to drink. To ease morning sickness, try drinking a cup of this tea as soon as you wake up and then again as needed throughout the day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tea is a natural remedy that can help ease morning sickness. The tea is made from the oat plant, which contains a special compound called avenanthramides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting. Avena sativa tea is also a natural relaxant, it can calm the stomach and reduce the feeling of queasiness. Many women who are pregnant and experiencing morning sickness have found relief by drinking avena sativa tea. The tea can also help regulate digestion and reduce stress, which can contribute to morning sickness. When consumed in small amounts, avena sativa tea is considered safe for pregnant women to drink and may be a useful addition to their morning routine.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Avenanthramides are good for morning sickness because they have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help to reduce nausea and alleviate the stomach discomfort associated with vomiting during pregnancy.
Saponins are good for morning sickness because they help to relax the stomach muscles, reduce nausea and vomiting, and calm the nervous system.
Tannins are good for morning sickness because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nausea and vomiting by calming the digestive system and reducing inflammation in the stomach.
Avenacosides are good for morning sickness because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea and vomiting during early pregnancy.
Ferulic acid is good for morning sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting by soothing the stomach.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea that can help with morning sickness, start by gathering dried chamomile flowers. You can find them in health food stores or online. Begin by measuring out one teaspoon of the dried flowers for every cup of boiling water you plan to use. Next, boil fresh water in a kettle or pot, then pour it over the dried chamomile flowers in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the water has cooled down to a comfortable temperature. Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the dried flowers, and add honey or lemon to taste if desired. Drink the tea as needed, ideally before or during meals, to help alleviate morning sickness symptoms. It's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using this herbal remedy, especially if you're pregnant or breastfeeding.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tea, also known as lemon balm tea, is a natural remedy for morning sickness. It has been used for centuries to help ease nausea and vomiting. The tea is made from the leaves of the lemon balm plant, which contains a chemical called rosmarinic acid. This acid has strong anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the stomach and reduce nausea. When you drink the tea, the rosmarinic acid is absorbed into your body and helps to relax the stomach muscles, making it easier to keep food down. Additionally, the tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate morning sickness. Many women who drink the tea report feeling better and being able to eat more without feeling sick. The tea is also safe to drink during pregnancy, making it a popular choice among expectant mothers.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for morning sickness because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce nausea and vomiting by calming the stomach and alleviating digestive discomfort.

Luteolin is good for morning sickness because it helps reduce nausea and vomiting by blocking the action of a chemical in the brain that causes these symptoms, resulting in relief for pregnant women experiencing morning sickness.
Apigenin is good for morning sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting by soothing the stomach and intestines.
Quercetin is good for morning sickness because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea for morning sickness relief, start by gathering dried chamomile flowers. Use one teaspoon of the dried flowers for every cup of boiling water. Next, boil fresh water in a kettle or on the stovetop, then carefully pour the hot water over the chamomile flowers in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, which will help release the herbal properties and flavors. After steeping, carefully pour the tea into a cup and discard the used chamomile flowers. You can add a bit of honey or sugar to taste, but be sure to drink it while still warm, as the heat will help in releasing the calming properties of the chamomile. Once you've made the tea, drink it as needed to help alleviate morning sickness symptoms, ideally when you wake up or before meals.

remedies.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla tea is often used to help with morning sickness. This is because it has natural ingredients that can calm the stomach and ease nausea. The tea contains a compound called apigenin, which can help relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation. This can help alleviate the symptoms of morning sickness, such as feeling queasy or having stomach cramps. Additionally, chamomilla tea has a soothing effect on the mind and body, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that often come with morning sickness. Drinking chamomilla tea may also help stabilize blood sugar levels and reduce dizziness. Many pregnant women have reported relief from morning sickness after drinking chamomilla tea, but it's always best to talk to a doctor before trying any new remedy.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Apigenin is good for morning sickness because it has been shown to reduce nausea and vomiting by blocking the action of hormones that trigger these symptoms.
Luteolin is good for morning sickness because it helps reduce nausea and vomiting by blocking chemical signals in the brain that trigger these symptoms.
Quercetin is good for morning sickness because it helps reduce inflammation and stabilizes the stomach, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by pregnancy hormones.

Tannins are good for morning sickness because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea, making them a natural remedy for pregnant women experiencing morning sickness.
Kaempferol is good for morning sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nausea and vomiting by calming the stomach and reducing stress hormones.

remedies.image_alt: Matricaria chamomilla herbal teas for morning sickness
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal tea that helps with morning sickness, start by gathering a few dried chamomile flowers. Use one teaspoon of dried flowers for every cup of boiling water. Place the flowers in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup and pour in the boiling water. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flowers to release their medicinal properties into the water. Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the flowers. You can add a little honey to taste, but it's best to drink it plain as it's gentle on the stomach. To help with morning sickness, drink a cup of the tea as soon as you wake up, and another before meals. You can also repeat this process throughout the day if you need relief from nausea.

supplementary_best: One combination that may help with morning sickness is a blend of Zingiber officinale (ginger), Foeniculum vulgare (fennel), and Avena sativa (oat straw). Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease nausea and stomach discomfort. Fennel is known to help alleviate digestive issues and reduce bloating, which can worsen morning sickness. Avena sativa, or oat straw, is said to have a balancing effect on the nervous system and may help reduce anxiety and stress, which can trigger nausea. This combination may be helpful because it targets multiple aspects of morning sickness, including digestive issues, inflammation, and anxiety.
supplementary_avoid: If you have morning sickness, it's best to avoid herbal teas that can worsen your symptoms. One of these teas is Yerba Mate, made from the leaves of the Ilex paraguariensis plant. Yerba Mate contains caffeine, which can increase nausea and vomiting. Another tea to avoid is Guarana, made from the seeds of the Paullinia cupana plant. Like Yerba Mate, Guarana contains high levels of caffeine, which can make morning sickness worse. Also, avoid teas that contain Peppermint, while peppermint may be calming for some, it can irritate the stomach and worsen nausea. Similarly, avoid teas that contain Passionflower (Passiflora incarnata), as it can cause drowsiness and exacerbate morning sickness symptoms.
supplementary_other_remedies: Ginger has been used for a long time to help with morning sickness. It's believed that the active ingredient in ginger, called gingerol, helps to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. When taken as a supplement or added to food, ginger can help to ease symptoms of morning sickness. Another natural remedy that may help is peppermint oil. When inhaled, the strong scent of peppermint oil can help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. Some women also find relief by applying peppermint oil to their wrists or temples. Vitamin B6 is another supplement that may help with morning sickness. It's believed that a lack of vitamin B6 can contribute to morning sickness, so taking a supplement can help to alleviate symptoms. Additionally, some women find that taking a probiotic supplement can help with morning sickness. Probiotics are good bacteria that help to keep the digestive system healthy, and may help to reduce nausea and vomiting.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience morning sickness during pregnancy often have other related health issues. One common problem is fatigue, which can be alleviated with medicinal herbs like ginger and peppermint. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting, while peppermint's calming effects can ease fatigue and promote relaxation. Another common issue is constipation, which can be relieved with herbs like dandelion and chamomile. Dandelion root has natural laxative properties that help stimulate bowel movements, while chamomile's soothing effects can calm the digestive system and reduce stress. Additionally, people with morning sickness often experience anxiety and mood swings, which can be managed with herbs like passionflower and wild yam. Passionflower's sedative properties help calm anxiety and promote relaxation, while wild yam's hormonal balancing effects can stabilize mood and reduce stress. These herbs can be consumed in the form of teas, capsules, or tinctures, and should be consulted with a healthcare professional before use.
